author: garyrong
content:
Can you elaborate it? 

Currently (before Amsterdam), we charge the CALL intrinsic cost (callee access cost, memory, transfer value, potential account-creation, delegate access), then forward 63/64 to child frame.

If child frame is failed due to (1) depth check (2) transfer balance check, the child frame is reverted without affecting the parent.

If we move the sanity checks into the parent frame, when processing the CALL opcode itself, will we halt the parent? or we silently ignore the error, aborting the child frame and continue? The latter one feels strange
